title: Number Bond Bridge Mini

source.notes: Third real concept run. Chosen to test a relation-and-decomposition style concept after combine_and_build (bakery-rush-mini) and sequence_and_order (snack-line-shuffle-mini). Also the first real run after Package 8 to confirm that request-derived timestamps remove the need for any timing coordination workaround.

concept_summary: A tiny K to 2 math game where the player completes a bridge by choosing the missing part in a number bond. The total is always visible, one part is given, and the player must choose the correct missing part so the two parts make the total exactly. A correct choice completes the bridge and lights it green. A wrong choice leaves the bridge incomplete and shows a gentle retry cue. No timer, no scoring, no multi-step levels.

core_mechanic.player_action: Look at the total and the given part, then choose the missing part that completes the number bond exactly.
core_mechanic.feedback_loop: The total and given part stay visible the whole time. A correct choice completes the bridge visually and highlights it green. A wrong choice shows a gentle retry cue while keeping the bond visible for another attempt.

prerequisites_candidate: understanding that two parts can make a whole

target_skill.description: Use part part whole reasoning to determine the missing addend within 10 instead of guessing from appearance or memorizing one surface pattern.

misconception_targets.description: Player treats the visible given part as the answer, or chooses a number that looks small or familiar, instead of reasoning about what missing part combines with the given part to make the total.

success_condition: The player chooses the correct missing part on first commit, showing they understand the relationship between the whole and the two parts rather than guessing.
constraints: one number bond per round in this first real run
the total must remain visible at all times
the given part must remain visible at all times
answer choices must all be plausible small numbers, not obviously wrong distractors
no timer pressure in this first real run
no scoring layer in this first real run
wrong answers must not punish the player; they should only trigger a retry cue

portfolio_fit.why_now: Third real concept run through the post-Package-8 backbone. Chosen to test whether the system handles a missing-component relation task cleanly after a composition task and an ordering task, and to confirm in practice that Package 8's request-derived timestamps remove the need for any timing coordination workaround.

next_action.reason: Use this brief as the third real concept run to confirm that request-derived timestamps remove the need for any timing workaround and that the enriched packet shape works on a missing-part concept.

unresolved_items.reason: No taxonomy/interaction_types.yaml entry exists for part-whole missing-component (number bond) tasks. The closest proven type combine_and_build is accumulation toward a target, which is the opposite cognitive act from decomposition. Leaving as 'unresolved' rather than forcing a bad label. See Friction #17.

No repo-grounded families.yaml entry exists for missing-component mini variants. Leaving as null rather than inventing a family name. Non-blocking at intake; should be revisited when a families taxonomy for part-whole games is introduced.

author_notes: Keep this run small and literal.
Do not add scoring, stars, or timer pressure.
Keep the visual representation simple so the missing-part reasoning is the real task.

Use this run mainly to confirm that Package 8 removed the timestamp workaround — pick ordinary chronological timestamps for brief, generation_request, and promotion_request and verify the written manifest and promotion timestamps mirror them exactly.
If this run stays clean, it becomes the third evidence point that the backbone is stabilizing across different interaction types.
